items.headline: Philippines declares national energy emergency as oil reserves drop to 45 days
items.body: The government declared a state of emergency after the Iran war disrupted imports from the Middle East, which supplies 98 percent of the country's oil. Nearly 400 gas stations have closed and 40,000 workers are stranded after deployment bans to Gulf states.

items.headline: China installs floating barrier across entrance to Scarborough Shoal
items.body: Satellite images show a 352-meter barrier blocking the disputed reef where Philippine fishermen operate under Chinese coast guard pressure. Diplomats warn China may be exploiting American focus on Iran to escalate South China Sea territorial claims.

items.headline: Sudan civil war enters fourth year as UN official calls it abandoned crisis
items.body: The conflict between government forces and the Rapid Support Forces has displaced a quarter of the population and left 19 million facing acute hunger. New evidence shows Ethiopia is providing military assistance to the paramilitaries despite official denials.

items.headline: IMF slashes Middle East growth forecast by nearly three percentage points
items.body: The region's expected growth dropped to 1.1 percent as oil exporters face Iran war fallout and multiple Gulf economies are now projected to contract. Iran's economy is forecast to shrink over six percent this fiscal year.

es.headline: Spain approves regularization program granting legal status to hundreds of thousands of undocumented migrants
es.body: Applicants must prove five months of residency and have no criminal record to receive one-year residence and work permits. The program contrasts sharply with tightening immigration policies across Europe as Spain faces labor shortages.

no.headline: Norway signs defense agreement with Ukraine establishing bilateral drone production in both countries
no.body: Ukraine will produce Norwegian-designed drones while Norway manufactures Ukrainian models under license, reversing the traditional donor-recipient dynamic. Norwegian forces will receive combat training from Ukrainian instructors who have operated drones in Europe's most intensive unmanned warfare environment.

uk.headline: IMF cuts UK growth forecast more than any major economy as Iran war drives inflation
uk.body: Britain's projected growth fell to less than one percent as fuel import costs surged sharply. Chancellor Rachel Reeves publicly criticized the US conflict as a mistake without a clear exit plan.

nl.headline: Netherlands refuses to join U.S. naval blockade of Strait of Hormuz
nl.body: Defense Minister Yeşilgöz announced Dutch forces will not participate in the American operation, calling it a worrying escalation. The government is instead coordinating with European allies and 10 other nations to restore free transit through the strategic waterway.
